subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 26
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is a mammal that lives in the water?
questions.explanation: Dolphins are mammals, meaning they breathe air, give birth to live young, and nurse their babies, unlike fish.
questions.hint: They are very smart and known for jumping out of the water.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Mexico?
questions.explanation: Mexico City is the capital and largest city of Mexico.
questions.hint: It shares its name with the country.

questions.q: Which part of a plant uses sunlight to make food?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll, which allows plants to convert sunlight into energy, a process called photosynthesis.
questions.hint: They are usually flat and green.

questions.q: What do we call the path a planet takes around the Sun?
questions.explanation: An orbit is the curved path of a celestial object or spacecraft around a star, planet, or moon.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit for your body?
questions.explanation: Getting enough sleep is essential for the brain and body to rest, grow, and function properly.
questions.hint: This helps you feel refreshed in the morning.

questions.q: Which community helper is trained to put out fires?
questions.explanation: Firefighters protect the community by extinguishing fires and responding to emergency situations.
questions.hint: They ride in big red trucks.

questions.q: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of the Earth's oceanic divisions.
questions.hint: It covers about 30% of the Earth's surface.

questions.q: Which gas makes up most of the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen accounts for approximately 78% of the Earth's atmosphere.
questions.hint: It is not the gas we breathe in.

questions.q: What is the force that pulls us toward the center of the Earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the natural force that attracts any object with mass toward the Earth.
questions.hint: It keeps our feet on the ground.

questions.q: Which of these is a musical instrument?
questions.explanation: A piano is a musical instrument that produces sound by striking strings with hammers.
questions.hint: It has black and white keys.
